headline: Dow Jones Futures Jump, Oil Prices Dive On Trump-Iran Cease-Fire; What To Do Now

headline: Delta beats on Q1 earnings, maintains guidance despite fuel spike, TSA headwinds
summary: Delta Air Lines posted upbeat first quarter results Wednesday morning, saying growth in the premium business would still bat away major concerns like fuel costs and the ongoing government shutdown affecting TSA workers.

content.title: Delta beats on Q1 earnings, maintains guidance despite fuel spike, TSA headwinds
content.summary: Delta Air Lines posted upbeat first quarter results Wednesday morning, saying growth in the premium business would still bat away major concerns like fuel costs and the ongoing government shutdown affecting TSA workers.

headline: US airline stocks climb premarket as oil prices tumble on Iran ceasefire
summary: Investing.com -- U.S. airline stocks jumped in premarket trading on Wednesday as oil prices plunged following a conditional ceasefire agreement between the United States and Iran, de-escalating a conflict that has gripped energy markets for more than five weeks.

content.title: US airline stocks climb premarket as oil prices tumble on Iran ceasefire

headline: Delta Baggage Fee Hike Highlights Fuel Shock And Revenue Mix Shift
summary: Delta Air Lines (NYSE:DAL) has raised checked baggage fees on domestic routes, its first such increase in two years. The move is intended to help offset higher jet fuel costs linked to the ongoing conflict in the Middle East. The change aligns Delta with recent baggage fee adjustments at United Airlines and JetBlue Airways. For you as an investor, this highlights how a large U.S. carrier that relies heavily on domestic and international passenger traffic is responding directly to external...

headline: Stocks to Watch: Levi Strauss, Delta Air Lines, Blue Owl

headline: Here Are the Stocks Rising the Most (and Some Losers)
summary: The market is rallying on hopes of a resumption of energy flows from the Middle East. Here are a few of the stocks moving the most as some of the big moves of recent weeks start to unwind. Going up: ✈️ Shares in Delta Air Lines (DAL), United Airlines (UAL) and Southwest Airlines (LUV), which have been walloped by higher fuel prices, are surging premarket.

content.title: Here Are the Stocks Rising the Most (and Some Losers)

Here are a few of the stocks moving the most as some of the big moves of recent weeks start to unwind. headline: Delta Air Lines Flags $2 Billion Iran Fuel Hit. Why the Stock Is Flying After Earnings.
summary: Delta Air Lines stock was surging ahead of the open Wednesday as the carrier’s earnings and the two-week U.S., Iran cease-fire deal proved to be tailwinds for the shares.  Demand is so strong that Delta expects to post a $1 billion pretax profit in the current quarter despite a more than $2 billion increase in fuel costs.  The carrier is also cutting capacity to mitigate the impact of higher fuel prices.

content.title: Delta Air Lines Flags $2 Billion Iran Fuel Hit. Why the Stock Is Flying After Earnings.
content.summary: Delta Air Lines stock was surging ahead of the open Wednesday as the carrier’s earnings and the two-week U.S., Iran cease-fire deal proved to be tailwinds for the shares.  Demand is so strong that Delta expects to post a $1 billion pretax profit in the current quarter despite a more than $2 billion increase in fuel costs.  The carrier is also cutting capacity to mitigate the impact of higher fuel prices.

headline: Delta (NYSE:DAL) Exceeds Q1 CY2026 Expectations, Stock Jumps 11.8%
summary: Global airline Delta Air Lines (NYSE:DAL) beat Wall Street’s revenue expectations in Q1 CY2026, with sales up 12.9% year on year to $15.85 billion. Its GAAP loss of $0.44 per share was significantly below analysts’ consensus estimates.

content.title: Delta (NYSE:DAL) Exceeds Q1 CY2026 Expectations, Stock Jumps 11.8%
content.summary: Global airline Delta Air Lines (NYSE:DAL) beat Wall Street’s revenue expectations in Q1 CY2026, with sales up 12.9% year on year to $15.85 billion. Its GAAP loss of $0.44 per share was significantly below analysts’ consensus estimates.
